questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: What is the current required to light a 60 W incandescent bulb in a domestic supply of 240 V ?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: An infinite combination of resistors, each having resistance $R = 4 \Omega$, is given below. What is the net resistance between the points A and B? (Each resistance is of equal value, $R = 4 \Omega$)
questions.solution:
Equivalent Resistance,

$$ \begin{array}{rlrl} y =\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{R}|| y \\\\ \Rightarrow y =\mathrm{R}+\frac{\mathrm{R} \times y}{\mathrm{R}+y} \\\\ \Rightarrow y =\frac{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{R} y+\mathrm{R} y}{\mathrm{R}+y} \\\\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{R} y+y^2 =\mathrm{R}^2+2 \mathrm{R} y \\\\ \Rightarrow y^2-4 y-16 =0 \\\\ \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{eq}} \text { or } y =2+2 \sqrt{5} \text { ohm } \end{array} $$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: For a human eye, where u is the distance of an object from the eye, f is the focal length of the lens and v is the distance of image from the eye, which is the correct schematic graph?
questions.solution: When object distance increase, in order to maintain image at retina, focal length increases initially, later for extreme distance it becomes constant. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Sand falls vertically on a conveyor belt at a work rate of 0⋅1 kg/s. In order to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s, the force required to be applied on the belt is :
questions.solution:
To determine the force required to keep the conveyor belt moving at a uniform speed while sand falls vertically onto it, you can use the relationship between force, mass flow rate, and velocity. Here are the steps to solve this problem:

Mass flow rate of sand:
$ \dot{m} = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} $

Velocity of the belt:
$ v = 2 \, \text{m/s} $

Momentum change per second (force required):
The force exerted by the falling sand on the belt is equal to the rate of change of momentum of the sand. Since the sand is falling vertically and starts moving horizontally with the belt upon landing, the horizontal force required can be calculated as:

$ F = \dot{m} \cdot v $

Substituting the given values:

$ F = 0.1 \, \text{kg/s} \cdot 2 \, \text{m/s} $

$ F = 0.2 \, \text{N} $

So, the force required to keep the belt moving at a uniform speed of 2 m/s is:

Option B: 0.2 N

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
questions.solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: The flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard. It verifies that
options: sound travels much faster than light
light travels much faster than sound
light and sound both travel with same speed
intensity of flash of lightning is very high during thunderstorm
questions.solution:
The phenomenon where the flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard can be explained by the difference in the speeds at which light and sound travel. Light travels significantly faster than sound. Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: light travels much faster than sound

To elaborate, light travels at an approximate speed of $$3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$$ in a vacuum, and slightly slower in the atmosphere. On the other hand, sound travels at a much slower speed of approximately $$343 \, \text{m/s}$$ in air at room temperature. Because of this large discrepancy in speeds, the light from the lightning is observed almost instantaneously, while the sound waves take a longer time to reach the observer's ears. This delay is why we see the flash of lightning before we hear the thunder.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: SONAR is a device that is used to measure the distance of underwater objects by a ship. Which of the following types of waves does it use for this purpose?
options: Sound waves in audible range for human beings
questions.solution:
SONAR, which stands for Sound Navigation and Ranging, is a device used to measure the distance of underwater objects. The correct type of waves that SONAR uses for this purpose is:

Option C: Ultrasonic waves

Ultrasonic waves are sound waves with frequencies higher than the audible range for human beings (greater than 20 kHz). These waves are particularly effective for underwater detection because they can travel long distances with high precision and can easily reflect off objects, providing accurate distance measurements.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: In which one among the following situations, the bulb ⊗ would glow the most ? (Consider all batteries are the same)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Power in a Bulb and Types of Circuits

Power, which makes a bulb glow, is found with the formula:

P = V2 / R

Here, P is power, V is voltage, and R is resistance.

In a series circuit, the battery's voltage is split between all the parts. In a parallel circuit, each part gets the full battery voltage.

EXPLANATION:

The bulb shines the brightest when it gets as much voltage as possible because more voltage means more power (according to the formula above).

If the bulb is in a parallel circuit, it will get the full battery voltage. This makes it glow more.

If the bulb is in a series circuit with other things, the voltage is shared, so the bulb gets less voltage and glows less.

So, the bulb will glow the most if it is connected in parallel.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements are correct about the electrical resistance and resistivity of a wire?

1. Both quantities depend on the area of cross-section of the wire

2. Both depend on the temperature

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire

4. Resistivity of the wire is directly proportional to the length of the
wire

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
To determine the correct statements about the electrical resistance and resistivity of a wire, let's analyze each statement individually.

1. Both quantities depend on the area of cross-section of the wire

This statement is partially correct. The resistance of a wire, given by the formula:

$$ R = \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

depends on the area of cross-section $$A$$, the length $$L$$, and the resistivity $$\rho$$ of the material. However, resistivity $$\rho$$ is an intrinsic property of the material and does not depend on the geometric factors such as the area of cross-section.

2. Both depend on the temperature

This statement is correct. Both resistance $$R$$ and resistivity $$\rho$$ of a wire generally depend on temperature. For most materials, resistivity changes with temperature, and since resistance is proportional to resistivity, resistance also changes with temperature.

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire

This statement is correct. From the equation:

$$ R = \rho \frac{L}{A} $$

it is clear that the resistance $$R$$ is directly proportional to resistivity $$\rho$$ when the length $$L$$ and cross-sectional area $$A$$ are kept constant.

4. Resistivity of the wire is directly proportional to the length of the wire

This statement is incorrect. Resistivity $$\rho$$ is an intrinsic property of the material and does not depend on the length of the wire. The resistivity remains the same regardless of the wire's length.

Based on the analysis, the correct statements are:

2. Both depend on the temperature.

3. Resistance of the wire is directly proportional to the resistivity of the wire.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C

2 and 3

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: The temperature of a body increases  from 310 K to 340 K. The temperature increase in degree Celsius is
questions.solution:
Temperature difference in kelvin and degree Celsius is the same.

Given:

$$ T_1 = 310\ \text{K}, \quad T_2 = 340\ \text{K} $$

Increase in temperature:

$$ \Delta T = T_2 - T_1 = 340 - 310 = 30\ \text{K} $$

So, the increase in degree Celsius is also:

$$ 30^\circ \text{C} $$

Correct option: 2. 30°C

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Evaporation from the surface of a given liquid takes place more rapidly when
options: the temperature is high and the surface area of the liquid is large
the temperature is low and the surface area of the liquid is large
the temperature is low and the surface area of the liquid is small
the temperature is high and the surface area of the liquid is small

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Suppose, a ball of mass M is thrown upwards from a point A and it reaches up to the highest point B and returns back to point A, which one among the following is correct ?
options: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at A
Kinetic Energy at B = Potential Energy at B
Kinetic Energy at B = Kinetic Energy at A
questions.solution:
The correct option is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Kinetic Energy at A = Potential Energy at B}}$$

Step-by-step explanation:

Let the ball be thrown upward from point $A$ with initial speed $u$.

At point $A$:

  • The ball has kinetic energy

$$KE_A = \frac{1}{2}Mu^2$$

  • If we take point $A$ as the reference level, then

$$PE_A = 0$$

At highest point $B$:

  • The velocity becomes zero, so

$$KE_B = 0$$

  • Let the height reached be $h$, then potential energy at $B$ is

$$PE_B = Mgh$$

Now, by conservation of mechanical energy:

$$\frac{1}{2}Mu^2 = Mgh$$

So,

$$KE_A = PE_B$$

Hence, Option 1 is correct.

Why other options are wrong:

  • Option 2: At point $A$, potential energy is usually taken as zero, so it is not equal to kinetic energy.

  • Option 3: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero but potential energy is not zero.

  • Option 4: At point $B$, kinetic energy is zero, but at $A$ it is maximum.

$$\boxed{\text{Correct Answer: Option 1}}$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about surface tension is correct ?
options: It will remain same with increase or decrease of temperature
It will always decrease with increase or decrease of temperature
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: It decreases when temperature increases.

Explanation:

Surface tension is the property of a liquid surface due to which it behaves like a stretched membrane.

When temperature increases, the molecules of the liquid gain more kinetic energy and move more freely. Because of this, the intermolecular force of attraction becomes less effective at the surface, so the surface tension decreases.

Therefore:

$$ \text{Surface tension decreases with increase in temperature} $$

So, the correct option is Option 2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.questionText: In which one of the following devices, the light energy is converted into the electrical energy?
questions.solution:
The device that converts light energy into electrical energy is the solar cell. Let's go through each option to understand why:

Option A: Light-emitting diode (LED)

A light-emitting diode converts electrical energy into light energy. When an electric current passes through an LED, it emits light due to the recombination of electrons and holes within the semiconductor material.

Option B: Laser diode

Similarly, a laser diode converts electrical energy into light energy, producing coherent light through stimulated emission when an electric current passes through it.

Option C: Solar cell

A solar cell, also known as a photovoltaic cell, converts light energy into electrical energy. When light photons hit the surface of a solar cell, they excite electrons and create electron-hole pairs. This generates a flow of electric current as these electrons move through the cell.

Option D: Transistor

A transistor is a semiconductor device that can amplify or switch electrical signals. It does not convert light energy into electrical energy.

Therefore, Option C: Solar cell is the correct answer.
